title: Buttondown vs Omnisend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Buttondown and Omnisend. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your needs best.
h1: Buttondown vs Omnisend: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: Buttondown and Omnisend are both freemium email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. Buttondown caters to developers and technical writers who value markdown simplicity, while Omnisend is purpose-built for e-commerce brands needing integrated SMS and email. Choosing between them depends on your industry, technical preferences, and channel requirements.
choose_a_if: You're a developer or technical writer who prefers writing in markdown
You're running a simple newsletter with under 100 subscribers on the free plan
You want the lowest-cost entry point at just $9/month for paid features
You need API access for custom integrations and automation
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce business and need email, SMS, and push notifications together
You want more generous free tier limits (250 subscribers vs 100)
You need pre-built workflows and advanced segmentation for complex campaigns
You're interested in their affiliate program to earn recurring commissions

faq.a: Neither is universally better—they excel in different areas. Buttondown is superior for technical writers who love markdown and want simplicity. Omnisend is better for e-commerce brands needing multi-channel capabilities like SMS and push notifications. Your choice depends on your specific use case and industry.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Buttondown or Omnisend?
faq.a: Buttondown has a lower entry price at $9/month compared to Omnisend's $16/month. However, Omnisend offers a more generous free tier (250 vs 100 subscribers), which may defer costs longer. Both have free plans, so you can test before paying.

faq.q: Can I switch from Omnisend to Buttondown?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import/export functionality, making migration possible. Buttondown explicitly lists import/export as a feature, allowing you to transfer your subscriber lists and data. Plan your migration during a low-send period to avoid disruption.
